title: First Go write route implementation gate

request.name: required string; trim before validation and update

transaction_and_state_invariants: Check tag existence before duplicate check and update.
Use a transaction for the update path.
Commit only after exactly one Tags row is updated.
Rollback leaves Tags unchanged on validation, missing tag, duplicate, and DB errors.
Only Tags.name may change on success.
No file path may be read, written, renamed, or deleted.

covered_cases: failure rollback leaves Tags and Note_Tags unchanged
default Go runtime remains query_only and rejects tag writes unless flag is enabled
parity_evidence.runtime_note: Current Python duplicate detection uses exact SELECT name matching in routes/tags.py. The docs/SCHEMA.md NOCASE wording is not expanded into a schema correction in 23.4.

not_authorized_by_23_4: Live Caddy route edit or reload
Go ownership of any route except local/copied-DB PUT /api/tags/<tag_id>
File upload, attachment, cleanup, import/export, migration, system, or server ownership

allowed_next_step.title: Go DB-only write expansion gate
allowed_next_step.scope: plan-only stabilization/selection for the next DB-only write route class after first-write local parity
recommended_focus: Stabilize tag rename evidence and decide whether live routing gate is needed before any broader DB-only writes.
Resolve or explicitly defer the Tags.name NOCASE schema/documentation discrepancy before tag CUD expansion.
Re-evaluate category create/update and note pin/archive without adding filesystem-coupled routes.
